user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the AppDynamics APM connector so that I can discover applications, tiers, nodes, and backend systems into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via basic auth using controller_url, account_name, username and password from the configuration_schema. With capabilities extraction:true and relationships:true it pulls four resource types (Applications, Application Tiers, Application Nodes, Backend Systems) and the relationships between them. incremental is false so each run is a full extraction; the manifest defines no default schedule, so cadence is driven by the discovery scheduler rather than the connector itself.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the AWS connector so that I can discover EC2 instances, RDS databases, S3 buckets, ECS/Lambda apps, cloud resources, and cost records into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with AWS IAM (aws_iam protocol) using the credential fields defined in authentication.fields (access key/role). Declares data_types infrastructure and financial and maps six resource types to CMDB CI types: virtual-machine, database, storage, application, cloud-resource and cost-record. capabilities.discovery is true while sync, real_time and incremental are false, so it performs full discovery on the default_schedule of every 6 hours (0 */6 * * *) within the rate limits of 10 req/s, 500 req/min, 10000 req/hr.

feature: Microsoft Azure cloud discovery & cost connector
user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Microsoft Azure connector so that I can discover virtual machines, databases, storage, applications, cloud resources, and cost records into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with an Azure service principal (azure_sp protocol). Declares data_types infrastructure and financial and discovers six resource types (virtual-machine, database, storage, application, cloud-resource, cost-record) plus a relationships block. capabilities.discovery is true with sync/real_time/incremental false, running full discovery on the default schedule of every 6 hours (0 */6 * * *).

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Azure AD (Entra ID) connector so that I can discover users, devices, enterprise applications, and groups into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via Azure OAuth2 client credentials using client_id, client_secret and tenant_id (with an include_guests toggle) from the configuration_schema. Extracts five resource types (Users, Devices, Enterprise Applications, Groups, Identity Relationships). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true and bidirectional:true, so it supports incremental syncs and write-back to Entra ID; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the BMC Helix ITSM connector so that I can discover servers, incidents, and changes into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via OAuth2 client credentials (oauth2_client_credentials). Declares data_types infrastructure and itil and ingests three resource types (server, incident, change). capabilities.incremental is true, so it performs incremental pulls on the default schedule of every 6 hours (0 */6 * * *), subject to the manifest rate_limits.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the BMC Remedy ITSM connector so that I can discover servers, incidents, and changes into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with basic auth (basic_auth). Declares data_types infrastructure and itil and ingests three resource types (server, incident, change). capabilities.incremental is true, running incremental pulls on the default schedule of every 6 hours (0 */6 * * *) within the configured rate limits.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Cisco Meraki Dashboard connector so that I can discover organizations, networks, devices, and clients into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with a Meraki Dashboard API key (plus base_url and optional organization_ids scoping) from the configuration_schema. Extracts four resource types (Organizations, Networks, Devices, Clients) with capabilities extraction:true and relationships:true. incremental and bidirectional are false, so every run is a full read-only discovery; no default schedule is set in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the CrowdStrike Falcon connector so that I can discover Falcon devices, threat detections, software vulnerabilities, and security incidents into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via OAuth2 client credentials using client_id and client_secret (with base_url and devices/detections toggles) from the configuration_schema. Extracts five resource types (Falcon Devices, Threat Detections, Software Vulnerabilities, Security Incidents, Security Relationships). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true, bidirectional:false, so it supports incremental read-only sync; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Datadog Monitoring connector so that I can discover infrastructure hosts, containers, APM services, and monitors into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with a Datadog api_key plus app_key against the configured site from the configuration_schema. Extracts four resource types (Infrastructure Hosts, Container Inventory, APM Services, Monitoring Definitions) with capabilities extraction:true and relationships:true. incremental and bidirectional are false, so each run is a full read-only extraction; no default schedule is set in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Microsoft Defender for Endpoint connector so that I can discover protected machines, security alerts, vulnerabilities, and installed software into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via Azure OAuth2 client credentials using client_id, client_secret and tenant_id (with machines/alerts/vulnerabilities toggles) from the configuration_schema. Extracts five resource types (Protected Machines, Security Alerts, Vulnerabilities, Installed Software, Defender Relationships). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true, bidirectional:false, so it supports incremental read-only sync; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Dell EMC Storage connector so that I can discover storage arrays, pools, volumes, and hosts into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via basic auth using username and password (authentication.type basic). Extracts four resource types (storage_arrays, storage_pools, volumes, hosts) with capabilities extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true, bidirectional:false. Unlike the v2 connectors, scheduling lives under a discovery block: a 3600-second interval, exponential retry policy (max 3 attempts), and rate limiting of 60 req/min with 5 concurrent requests, plus a health_check GET against /api/types/system/instances expecting HTTP 200.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Docker connector so that I can discover containers, applications, storage, networks, and host servers into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authentication is optional (authentication.required false; methods basic or none) since it talks to a local/remote Docker daemon. Discovers five resource types (container, application, storage, network-device, server). capabilities.discovery is true with sync/real_time/incremental false, so it runs full discovery on the default schedule of every hour (0 */1 * * *) within the manifest rate limits.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Dynatrace APM connector so that I can discover hosts, processes, services, and applications into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with a Dynatrace api_token against the configured environment_url from the configuration_schema. Extracts four resource types (Hosts, Processes, Services, Applications) with capabilities extraction:true and relationships:true. incremental and bidirectional are false, so each run is a full read-only extraction; no default schedule is set in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Freshservice connector so that I can discover assets, incidents, and changes into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with an API key (api_key method). Declares data_types infrastructure and itil and ingests three resource types (asset, incident, change). capabilities.incremental is true, running incremental pulls on the default schedule of every 4 hours (0 */4 * * *) within the configured rate limits.

feature: Google Cloud Platform discovery & cost connector
user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Google Cloud Platform connector so that I can discover virtual machines, databases, storage, cloud resources, applications, and cost records into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with a GCP service account (gcp_sa protocol). Declares data_types infrastructure and financial and discovers six resource types (virtual-machine, database, storage, cloud-resource, application, cost-record). capabilities.discovery is true with sync/real_time/incremental false, so it runs full discovery on the default schedule of every 6 hours (0 */6 * * *).

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Microsoft Hyper-V connector so that I can discover virtual machines, host servers, network devices, and storage into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates over WinRM (winrm protocol) using the configured Windows credentials. Discovers four resource types (virtual-machine, server, network-device, storage). capabilities.discovery is true with sync/real_time/incremental false, so it performs full discovery on the default schedule of every 4 hours (0 */4 * * *) within the manifest rate limits.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the IBM Cloud connector so that I can discover virtual server instances, databases, block storage, and VPC networks into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with an IBM Cloud api_key scoped by region and resource_group_id from the configuration_schema. Extracts five resource types (Virtual Server Instances, Database Instances, Block Storage Volumes, VPC Networks, Resource Relationships). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true, bidirectional:false, so it supports incremental read-only sync; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Infoblox IPAM/DNS connector so that I can discover networks, host records, DNS records, and DHCP ranges into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via basic auth (username/password) against the Grid Master WAPI using grid_master_url, wapi_version and verify_ssl from the configuration_schema. Extracts four resource types (Networks, Host Records, DNS Records, DHCP Ranges) with capabilities extraction:true and relationships:true. incremental and bidirectional are false, so each run is a full read-only extraction; no default schedule is set in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Microsoft Intune connector so that I can discover managed devices, managed apps, compliance policies, configuration profiles, and users into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via Azure OAuth2 client credentials using client_id, client_secret and tenant_id from the configuration_schema. Extracts six resource types (Managed Devices, Managed Applications, Compliance Policies, Configuration Profiles, Managed Users, Device and App Relationships). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true, bidirectional:false, so it supports incremental read-only sync; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the JAMF Pro connector so that I can discover computers, mobile devices, applications, and policies into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via basic auth (username/password) against jamf_url, with a use_classic_api toggle and computers/mobile_devices selection flags from the configuration_schema. Extracts four resource types (Computers, Mobile Devices, Applications, Policies). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true, bidirectional:false, so it supports incremental read-only sync; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Jira Asset Management connector so that I can discover Jira asset objects into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with a Jira API token using email, api_token and instance_url, scoped by asset_schema_id and an optional jql_filter from the configuration_schema. capabilities are extraction:true and relationships:true (incremental/bidirectional false). However, the manifest declares no resources[] array, so despite being named 'Jira Asset Management' the connector defines zero resource types to ingest and would discover nothing as shipped.
issue_note: packages/connectors/jira/connector.json has no resources key (.resources is null) — the connector advertises asset discovery but defines no resource types, so it cannot ingest any CIs.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Jira Service Management connector so that I can discover assets, incidents, and changes into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with an API token (api_token method). Declares data_types infrastructure and itil and ingests three resource types (asset, incident, change). capabilities.incremental is true, running incremental pulls on the default schedule of every 4 hours (0 */4 * * *) within the configured rate limits.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Kubernetes connector so that I can discover nodes, applications, and services into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates against the cluster API using the kubernetes auth method (kubeconfig/service-account token). Discovers nodes mapped to server CIs plus application and service resources. capabilities.discovery is true with sync/real_time/incremental false, so it runs full discovery on the default schedule of every 2 hours (0 */2 * * *). Note the resources array lists 'application' twice (workloads and a second application entry), which is a minor redundancy rather than a defect.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Lansweeper Asset Manager connector so that I can discover servers, workstations, network devices, and software inventory into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with an api_key against api_url, scoped by site_id from the configuration_schema. Extracts five resource types (Servers, Workstations, Network Devices, Software Inventory, Asset Relationships). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true, bidirectional:false, so it supports incremental read-only sync; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the NetApp ONTAP connector so that I can discover storage nodes, volumes, LUNs, and aggregates into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via basic auth (username/password) against cluster_url with verify_ssl from the configuration_schema. Extracts five resource types (Storage Nodes, Storage Volumes, LUNs, Aggregates, Storage Relationships). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true, bidirectional:false, so it supports incremental read-only sync; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Nutanix connector so that I can discover clusters, hosts, virtual machines, and storage containers into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via basic auth (authentication.type basic) using prism_url, username and password, with a prism_type select for Prism Central (multi-cluster) vs Prism Element (single cluster). Extracts four resource types (clusters, hosts, virtual_machines, storage_containers). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true and bidirectional:true; a healthcheck POSTs to /api/nutanix/v3/clusters/list expecting HTTP 200, and configuration exposes polling_interval, rate_limit, timeout and max_retries.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Okta Identity Management connector so that I can discover users, applications, devices, and groups into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with an Okta api_token (SSWS) against okta_domain, with include_deactivated and a sync_direction option from the configuration_schema. Extracts five resource types (Users, Applications, Devices, Groups, Identity Relationships). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true and bidirectional:true, so it supports incremental sync and write-back governed by sync_direction; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Red Hat OpenShift connector so that I can discover clusters, nodes, projects/namespaces, and deployments into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates against api_url either with a bearer token or username/password (plus verify_ssl) from the configuration_schema. Extracts five resource types (OpenShift Clusters, Cluster Nodes, Projects/Namespaces, Application Deployments, Resource Relationships). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true and bidirectional:true, so it supports incremental sync and write-back; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure connector so that I can discover compute instances, database systems, block storage, and virtual cloud networks into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with an OCI API signing key using tenancy_ocid, user_ocid, fingerprint, private_key and region, scoped by compartment_id from the configuration_schema (the manifest also declares credential_affinity). Extracts seven resource types: Compute Instances, Database Systems, Block Storage Volumes, Virtual Cloud Networks, Volume Attachments, VNIC Attachments and Database Homes. capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true, bidirectional:false, so it supports incremental read-only sync; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Palo Alto Prisma Cloud connector so that I can discover cloud accounts, cloud assets, vulnerabilities, and compliance findings into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with access_key and secret_key against api_url, optionally filtered by cloud_types, from the configuration_schema. Extracts five resource types (Cloud Accounts, Cloud Assets, Asset Vulnerabilities, Compliance Findings, Asset Relationships). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true, bidirectional:false, so it supports incremental read-only sync; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Prometheus connector so that I can discover monitored targets, service discovery, alerts, and metrics into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Connects to prometheus_url with optional HTTP basic auth (basic_auth_username/basic_auth_password) and a timeout_ms from the configuration_schema. Extracts four resource types (Monitored Targets, Service Discovery, Active Alerts, Available Metrics) with capabilities extraction:true and relationships:true. incremental and bidirectional are false, so each run is a full read-only extraction; no default schedule is set in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Proxmox VE connector so that I can discover virtual machines, containers, host servers, storage, and network devices into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates using the proxmox auth method (API token/ticket against the Proxmox VE API). Discovers five resource types (virtual-machine, container, server, storage, network-device). capabilities.discovery is true with sync/real_time/incremental false, so it runs full discovery on the default schedule of every 3 hours (0 */3 * * *) within the manifest rate limits.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Rancher Kubernetes Management connector so that I can discover Kubernetes clusters, nodes, projects, and workloads into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with access_key and secret_key (Rancher API key) against rancher_url, optionally scoped by cluster_ids, with a sync_direction option from the configuration_schema. Extracts five resource types (Kubernetes Clusters, Cluster Nodes, Rancher Projects, Kubernetes Workloads, Rancher Relationships). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true and bidirectional:true, so it supports incremental sync and write-back; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Rubrik CDM connector so that I can discover clusters, protected VMs, protected physical hosts, and SLA domains into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via basic auth (username/password) against cluster_url with api_version and verify_ssl from the configuration_schema. Extracts five resource types (Rubrik Clusters, Protected Virtual Machines, Protected Physical Hosts, SLA Domains, Backup Relationships) with capabilities extraction:true and relationships:true. incremental and bidirectional are false, so each run is a full read-only extraction; no default schedule is set in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Microsoft SCCM connector so that I can discover managed devices, software inventory, device collections, and software updates into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Connects to the SCCM SQL database using server, database and site_code, authenticating with either Windows auth (use_windows_auth) or username/password from the configuration_schema. Extracts four resource types (Managed Devices, Software Inventory, Device Collections, Software Updates) with capabilities extraction:true and relationships:true. incremental and bidirectional are false, so each run is a full read-only extraction; no default schedule is set in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the ServiceNow CMDB & ITSM connector so that I can discover servers, virtual machines, databases, applications, network devices, plus incidents and changes into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via basic auth (username/password) against instance_url with a sync_direction option from the configuration_schema. Declares data_types infrastructure and itil and extracts eight resource types: Servers, Virtual Machines, Databases, Applications, Network Devices, CI Relationships, incident and change. capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true and bidirectional:true, so it supports incremental sync and bidirectional write-back governed by sync_direction; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Tanium Endpoint Management connector so that I can discover managed endpoints, installed software, missing patches, and custom sensor data into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via basic auth (username/password) against server_url, optionally scoped by computer_group, with a sync_direction option from the configuration_schema. Extracts five resource types (Managed Endpoints, Installed Software, Missing Patches, Custom Sensor Data, Endpoint Relationships). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true and bidirectional:true, so it supports incremental sync and write-back; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Tenable.io Vulnerability Management connector so that I can discover devices, vulnerabilities, assets, and plugins into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with access_key and secret_key against api_url with verify_ssl from the configuration_schema. Extracts five resource types (Devices, Vulnerabilities, Assets, Plugins, Vulnerability Relationships). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true, bidirectional:false, so it supports incremental read-only sync; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Veeam Backup & Replication connector so that I can discover backup servers, protected VMs, backup jobs, and repositories into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via basic auth (username/password) against enterprise_manager_url with verify_ssl from the configuration_schema. Extracts four resource types (Backup Servers, Protected Virtual Machines, Backup Jobs, Backup Repositories) with capabilities extraction:true and relationships:true. incremental and bidirectional are false, so each run is a full read-only extraction; no default schedule is set in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the VMware vSphere connector so that I can discover virtual machines, ESXi hosts, datastores, and networks into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via basic auth (vCenter username/password). Discovers five resource types (virtual-machine, server for ESXi hosts, cloud-resource, storage for datastores, network-device). capabilities.discovery is true with sync/real_time/incremental false, so it performs full discovery on the default schedule of every 4 hours (0 */4 * * *) within the manifest rate limits.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Wiz Cloud Security connector so that I can discover cloud resources, vulnerabilities, security issues, and cloud identities into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via OAuth2 client credentials using client_id and client_secret against auth_url and api_url, with cloud_resources/vulnerabilities/issues/identities selection toggles from the configuration_schema. Extracts five resource types (Cloud Resources, Vulnerabilities, Security Issues, Cloud Identities, Wiz Relationships). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true, bidirectional:false, so it supports incremental read-only sync; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Zabbix Monitoring connector so that I can discover hosts, services, applications, triggers, and host groups into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates via basic auth (username/password) against server_url, optionally scoped by host_groups, with a sync_direction option from the configuration_schema. Extracts six resource types (Hosts, Services, Applications, Triggers, Host Groups, Host Relationships). capabilities are extraction:true, relationships:true, incremental:true and bidirectional:true, so it supports incremental sync and write-back; no default schedule is defined in the manifest.

user_story: As an integration admin, I want to install and run the Zendesk connector so that I can discover incidents (support tickets) into the CMDB.
expected_behavior: Authenticates with an API token (api_token method). Declares data_type itil and ingests a single resource type (incident/ticket). capabilities.incremental is true, running incremental pulls on the default schedule of every 4 hours (0 */4 * * *) within the configured rate limits.
